name: token-optimizer-statusline
description: See your Claude Code or GitHub Copilot context health, efficiency, and usage limits in the VS Code status bar.

enumDescriptions: Detect automatically: read whichever runtime (Claude Code or GitHub Copilot) you've used most recently. Pure-Copilot and pure-Claude setups just work; no setting to flip.
Always read cache files written by the Claude Code Token Optimizer plugin (~/.claude/token-optimizer/).
Always read cache files written by the GitHub Copilot Token Optimizer install (~/.copilot/token-optimizer/). Requires a Copilot Token Optimizer installation.
tokenOptimizer.runtime.markdownDescription: Which AI coding assistant runtime to read Token Optimizer cache data from. **auto** (default) picks whichever of Claude Code or GitHub Copilot you used most recently, so Copilot users don't have to change anything. Set it explicitly to `claude` or `copilot` to pin one. Changing this setting takes effect immediately without reloading VS Code.

tokenOptimizer.staleAfterSeconds.markdownDescription: How many seconds before verified statusline usage-limit data is shown as cached. Cached values show their update age; manual refresh also tries a local transcript estimate.
